skill.name: ecommerce-livestream-overlay-generator
skill.description: Generates e-commerce livestream overlay assets from brand, product, promotion, and optional product photo details. It guides copywriting, image prompts, green screen removal, compositing, and delivery packaging for a complete visual set.
skill.summary: Generate a complete e-commerce livestream overlay set with prompts, compositing guidance, and delivery packaging.
skill.author: gaoyuji12138

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many backticks, code fences, and model names as command execution or weak cryptography; most of those are false positives in markdown examples. Real medium-risk behavior remains because the skill instructs use of an external image API with bearer authentication, provides executable image-processing scripts, writes local output files, and includes wording that appears to avoid audit-sensitive trust claims.

medium_findings.title: External Image API With Bearer Authentication
medium_findings.description: The skill documents a POST request to an external image generation endpoint and includes a bearer authorization header placeholder. This is expected for the stated functionality, but prompts, product photos, and API credentials may leave the local environment if users run the example without reviewing data handling.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network endpoint and Authorization header are explicit. The risk is medium because external API use is core to the skill, but users must supply credentials and may send commercial product data.

medium_findings.title: Executable Local Image Processing and Packaging Instructions
medium_findings.description: The skill provides Python snippets that read and write image files, plus shell commands for dependency installation and zip packaging. These examples are legitimate for image generation workflows, but they require local execution privileges and should be reviewed before use on untrusted images or user-controlled brand names.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands and scripts are present in fenced examples and align with the skill purpose. The concern is operational risk from executing local file operations, not confirmed malicious code.

medium_findings.title: Audit-Evasive Marketing Copy Guidance
medium_findings.description: The copywriting section tells users to avoid audit-triggering words such as trust claim terms and substitute softer wording. This is a semantic marketplace risk because it may encourage avoiding review of commercial claims instead of making claims accurate and compliant.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The phrase about audit-triggering words is explicit and has direct marketplace relevance. It does not prove malware intent, but it is a clear policy and trust-risk signal.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography alerts point to prose, tables, prompt examples, dimensions, and model names. I did not find evidence of cryptographic code, hashing, encryption, or weak algorithm use in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of representative flagged lines shows markdown content and workflow prose, not crypto APIs. No evidence found for weak cryptographic algorithm use.

low_findings.title: Most Backtick Command Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Many external command findings map to markdown fences, inline model names, prompt templates, and prose rather than executed code. Real command examples are captured separately as medium-risk operational guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited areas are plainly fenced examples or markdown template text. They are not runtime code paths in this skill package.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Alert Is a Packaging Pattern
low_findings.description: The zip command writes one level above the output directory using a relative parent path. This is not confirmed traversal abuse, but the brand placeholder should be sanitized before being used in filenames.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The ../ segment is visible and explains the static alert. The surrounding context is normal packaging, so the risk depends on how the placeholder is resolved.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bearer Token Curl Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill shows a curl request with an Authorization bearer header to an external image service. Users should keep API keys out of logs and shared transcripts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The bearer header and external URL are explicit. The token is a placeholder, so this is a handling risk rather than leaked credentials.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Read Write Scripts
dangerous_patterns.description: The Python snippets read image paths, process pixels, and write output images. This is expected behavior, but it should be run only on trusted paths and reviewed images.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are visible in the snippets. They are standard image-processing operations with no hidden network or credential access.

content.value_statement: Livestream sellers need complete visual packages that match product, promotion, and brand style. This skill turns those inputs into structured prompts, image-processing steps, and delivery guidance for overlay assets.
actual_capabilities: Collects brand, product, promotion, and optional style details before generation.
Creates prompt templates for background, title banner, host persona, pricing card, product shelf, and benefits bar.
Provides Python examples for product background removal, green screen removal, compositing, and brightness adjustment.
Defines image sizes, layering order, output filenames, and packaging steps for seven deliverables.
Supports workflows that use a provided product photo in the product shelf composition.
limitations: Requires access to a compatible external image generation API and a valid API key.
Does not include a finished application or automated runner in the repository.
Generated product and host visuals still need human review for accuracy and brand compliance.
Local scripts and filenames should be reviewed before use with untrusted images or user-provided brand names.

use_cases.title: Prepare Campaign Visuals
use_cases.description: Create a full overlay asset plan for a seasonal product promotion before a live shopping event.

use_cases.title: Standardize Creative Production
use_cases.description: Use fixed component sizes, naming rules, and prompt patterns to keep recurring livestream assets consistent.

use_cases.title: Adapt Product Photos
use_cases.description: Integrate a real product photo into a generated shelf layout and composite preview.
use_cases.target_user: Small brand owners and livestream hosts

prompt_templates.title: Start With Required Inputs
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a standard overlay request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a livestream overlay set for [brand]. Featured product: [product]. Promotion: [offer]. Style preference: [optional style]. Confirm the brief before creating prompts.

prompt_templates.title: Create Component Prompts
prompt_templates.scenario: Build all asset prompts after requirements are confirmed.
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the confirmed brief, write prompts for the background, title banner, host persona, pricing card, product shelf, and benefits bar. Keep style consistent across all components.

prompt_templates.title: Use a Product Photo
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with a provided product image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use my product photo as the featured item. Plan background removal, reserve center shelf space, place the product in the composite, and list any quality checks needed.

prompt_templates.title: Package Final Deliverables
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced delivery preparation after assets are generated.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a delivery checklist for the seven final livestream overlay files. Include file names, formats, preview order, and packaging method for my channel.

output_examples.input: Brand: Dyson. Product: Supersonic hair dryer and Airwrap. Promotion: discount with free styling nozzles.
output: A confirmed creative brief with main title, subtitle, pricing tiers, and benefits copy.
Six component prompt directions for a technology-luxury livestream overlay set.
A delivery checklist for preview image and packaged overlay files.

output_examples.input: A product photo with only a request to make a livestream overlay.
output: A request for confirmation of inferred brand, product category, and promotion details.
A product shelf plan that reserves center space for the provided photo.
Background removal and compositing steps for the supplied image.

output_examples.input: A cosmetics promotion needs a premium visual style and a host persona.
output: A coordinated palette, host styling direction, and component prompt set.
A layered preview plan covering background, host, pricing card, title, product shelf, and benefits bar.

best_practices: Confirm brand, product, and promotion details before generating image prompts.
Review all commercial claims for accuracy before using generated marketing copy.
Use sanitized filenames and protect API keys when running examples.
anti_patterns: Do not paste real API keys into shared chats, logs, or public files.
Do not claim official status, authenticity, warranties, or guarantees without proof.
Do not run local scripts on untrusted image paths without reviewing the code first.

faq.question: What inputs does this skill need?
faq.answer: It needs a brand, featured product, and promotion. Style preference and product photos are optional.

faq.question: Does it generate final images by itself?
faq.answer: It provides prompts, API request guidance, processing scripts, and delivery steps. It depends on external image generation access.

faq.question: Can it use a real product photo?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for background removal and placement in the product shelf composite.

faq.question: What files does the workflow produce?
faq.answer: The workflow defines six overlay components and one composite preview image.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It has medium operational risk. Users should review external API use, local scripts, filenames, and marketing claims.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
